title: Saint Catherine’s Monastery
description: World’s oldest continuously inhabited monastery (6th Century), UNESCO site. St. Catherine.

title: Chapel of the Burning Bush
description: Sacred heart of the monastery, site of Moses’ biblical vision. St. Catherine.

title: Mount Sinai (Gebel Musa)
description: Holy mountain with the "Summit Mosque" and "Chapel of the Holy Trinity". St. Catherine.

title: Deir el-Arbain
description: Monastery of the Forty Martyrs, isolated site with olive groves and martyr history. St. Catherine.

title: Chapel of St. John Climacus
description: Cave chapel dedicated to the author of "The Ladder of Divine Ascent". St. Catherine.

title: Elijah’s Basin
description: Plateau on Mount Sinai with an ancient chapel and giant cypress tree. St. Catherine.

title: Gebel Katherina Summit
description: Egypt’s highest point, featuring a chapel dedicated to St. Catherine. St. Catherine.

title: Temple of Hathor (Serabit el-Khadim)
description: Unique pharaonic temple to the "Lady of Turquoise" atop a mountain. Serabit el-Khadim.

title: Wadi Maghara Inscriptions
description: Oldest turquoise mines with Old Kingdom rock reliefs of kings smiting enemies. Wadi Maghara.

title: Wadi Kharig
description: Pharaonic mining site with significant inscriptions and rock stelae. Near Serabit.

title: Bir Nasib
description: Ancient copper mining site with massive slag heaps and inscriptions. Near Serabit.

title: Salah El-Din Citadel (Pharaoh’s Island)
description: Strategic sea fortress defending the Gulf of Aqaba. Taba.

title: Fortress of El-Gindi
description: Inland Ayyubid fortress built by Saladin to secure pilgrimage routes. Ras Sudr.

title: Raithu Monastery Walls
description: Remains of the ancient Raithu fortress and monastery walls. El-Tor.

title: El-Kilani Neighborhood
description: Historic district with coral-stone architecture and Ottoman houses. El-Tor.

title: Tell Mahrad (Ancient Pharan)
description: Ruins of the ancient capital of Sinai, with a cathedral and monastic remains. Wadi Feiran.

title: Deir el-Banat
description: Ruins of an ancient convent dating to the early Christian era. Wadi Feiran.

title: Nun’s Convent (Feiran)
description: Functioning convent built amongst the ancient ruins of Pharan. Wadi Feiran.

title: Gebel Tahuna
description: Mountain with ruins of ancient churches, hermit cells, and crosses. Wadi Feiran.

title: Wadi Mukattab
description: The "Valley of Writings", filled with Nabataean, Greek, and hieroglyphic inscriptions. Wadi Mukattab.

title: Rod el-Air
description: Ancient approach to Serabit containing rock carvings and inscriptions. Near Serabit.

title: Hajar el-Maktub
description: Major site for Nabataean inscriptions in Wadi Aleyat. Base of Gebel Serbal.

title: Ein Hudra Nawamis
description: Well-preserved prehistoric circular stone tombs (Bronze Age). Ein Hudra.

title: Gebel Gunna Nawamis
description: Spectacular field of prehistoric Nawamis structures. Near Ein Hudra.

title: White Canyon Sites
description: Archaeological remains and water sources within the canyon. Near Ein Hudra.

title: Sheikh Awad
description: Islamic shrines and nearby prehistoric Nawamis tombs. Central Sinai.

title: Wadi Shellal
description: Ancient shell middens and mining route markers. Red Sea Coast.

title: Ain Gharandal
description: Remains of a Roman castellum protecting a water source. Ras Sudr Road.

title: Hammam Pharaon
description: Ancient hot springs and hillside caves with historical association. Ras Sudr Area.

title: Ain Khudra
description: Historic oasis and station for ancient pilgrims. East Sinai.

title: Gebel Serbal
description: Mountain with early monastic settlements and panoramic significance. Wadi Feiran.
